articles.title: Man convicted more than 3 decades after raping 2 women at gunpoint in Framingham store
articles.description:
Stephen Paul Gale, 73, evaded police for over three decades until his capture in 2024, the DA said.

The post Man convicted more than 3 decades after raping 2 women at gunpoint in Framingham store appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, convicted for raping two women at gunpoint in Framingham store.
Gale evaded capture for over 30 years before being arrested in 2024.
The conviction brings closure to victims and highlights ongoing public safety concerns.

articles.title: New Providence basketball coach goes viral for recruiting joke: ‘Just look at my wife’
articles.description:
"If you ever want to question my recruiting abilities, just look at my wife, Jordan."

The post New Providence basketball coach goes viral for recruiting joke: ‘Just look at my wife’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Providence basketball coach Bryan Hodgson went viral for a humorous recruiting joke.
Hodgson's joke featured his wife, Jordan, highlighting his recruiting confidence.
The viral moment boosts local interest in Providence basketball among Boston residents.

articles.title: He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care
articles.description:
"I was lucky enough to be born into an amazing one. But, family is also the people who pick you, show up for you, and love you. On Marathon Monday, I'll be running for every kid, who deserves to feel that, too."

The post He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Alex Castrichini will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to support children in foster care.
He emphasizes the importance of chosen family for kids in need of support.
Funds raised will directly benefit local foster care programs in Boston.

articles.title: ‘Children’s lives are at stake’: Boston teachers protest staffing cuts
articles.description:
“We're losing support staff," a South End educator said. "If anything, you should be putting more supports in these classes, not taking away."

The post ‘Children’s lives are at stake’: Boston teachers protest staffing cuts appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston teachers protested staffing cuts affecting student support services on March 24.
South End educator emphasized the need for more support staff in classrooms.
Reduced staffing may negatively impact children's education and well-being in Boston.

articles.title: Bruins fall to Maple Leafs 4-2 at TD Garden
articles.description:
Jeremy Swayman had 31 saves for the Bruins, who lost at home for just the second time in their last 16 games.

The post Bruins fall to Maple Leafs 4-2 at TD Garden appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bruins lost 4-2 to Maple Leafs at TD Garden on March 24.
Jeremy Swayman recorded 31 saves during the game.
This marks the Bruins' second home loss in 16 games.

articles.title: Where ‘No Kings’ protests are happening around Massachusetts this Saturday
articles.description:
From Boston Common to nearby suburbs, here’s where and when rallies are scheduled for March 28’s “No Kings” protests.

The post Where ‘No Kings’ protests are happening around Massachusetts this Saturday appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Protests against monarchy scheduled in Boston Common and suburbs on March 28.
Rallies will occur at multiple locations across Massachusetts, mobilizing local residents.
Participants aim to raise awareness about democratic values and local governance.
